response.justification: While the candidate meets the minimum education and experience threshold (Bachelor's in CS + 14 years of professional experience), this is a full-stack software engineer with no demonstrated expertise in the core technical domain. The role requires SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, AI accelerator optimization, and deep learning workload expertise. The candidate's background is exclusively in web services, full-stack development, and microservices, with no evidence of hardware architecture design, performance optimization for specialized hardware, or deep learning.
strengths: Meets the education and years-of-experience floor (Bachelor's in CS + 14 years post-graduation)
Proven ability to optimize performance-critical systems (low-latency apps handling millions of requests/second, pricing strategy optimization)
Software craftsmanship fundamentals with TDD, CI/CD, and system design experience across multiple technical domains
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience—the fundamental technical domain required for this role
Background is exclusively web and full-stack engineering; no hardware architecture, chip design, or specialized accelerator optimization work
Current AI exposure (re:cinq) is AI-agent infrastructure tooling, not hardware-level neural network or deep learning optimizer expertise

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and GPU architecture expertise
No deep learning or AI accelerator optimization background
General software engineering maturity and years of experience
